title: Resulting $M$ is a closed manifold without boundary (boundary cancellation \& smooth structure)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The problem demands a **compact manifold without boundary**. Stage B introduces boundaries $\partial W_F=S(V)$; these must exactly cancel the boundaries created by excising the cone neighborhoods.

**Obligation.** (a) Verify dimension/parity matching so the glued object is a manifold of the expected dimension. (b) Prove the smooth (or topological) structure extends across every gluing seam (collar arguments). (c) Conclude $\widetilde M$, hence $M=\widetilde M/\Gamma$, is boundaryless and compact. Any leftover boundary or non-extendable chart breaks the realization.
